description: The pie chart only shows 5 of the 15 candidates, but the percentage labels are calculated relative to the total disbursements of all 15 candidates — so the slices sum to only about 68%, leaving viewers confused about where the remaining 32% went. A pie chart inherently implies that the displayed slices represent a complete whole (100%), so readers will incorrectly assume they are seeing the full picture. This misrepresents how campaign spending is distributed among all candidates.
